latest.title: Half-yearly results for the six months ended 30 November 2025
latest.content:
Today 07:03
Karelian Diamond Resources Plc - Half-yearly results for the six months ended 30 November 2025
Karelian Diamond Resources Plc - Half-yearly results for the six months ended 30 November 2025
PR Newswire
LONDON, United Kingdom, February 27
27 February 2026
Karelian Diamond Resources plc
(“Karelian” or “the Company”)
Half-yearly results for the six months ended 30 November 2025
Karelian Diamond Resources plc (AIM: KDR), the diamond exploration company focused on Finland, announces its unaudited results for the six months ended 30 November 2025.
Details of these can be found below and a full copy of the interim results statement can be viewed on the Company’s website
(
www.kareliandiamondresources.com
).
Highlights of the half-year period included:
In Northern Ireland the Company holds a significant licence package covering over 1000 km² and has established first mover advantage. It continues to advance its exploration programme for Nickel, Copper and Platinum Group Elements.
Exploration work also continued in the Kuhmo region in Finland with the Company focussing in particular on the geophysical Anomaly 5 as a priority. It lies up-ice of the green-diamond find.
At the Lahtojoki diamond deposit Karelian was, in June, granted a mining concession certificate and the focus of the Company’s efforts over the last number of months have been on advancing discussions with a view to securing appropriate strategic or partnership finance to progress development.
Brendan McMorrow, Chairman of Karelian Diamonds, stated:
“The Company is looking forward to progressing with the work programmes on its highly prospective Nickel, Copper and Platinum Group Element targets in Northern Ireland (including a tangible Copper target) and a clear work programme and strategy has been identified for its exploration assets in Finland.
The Board remains confident regarding the prospects for the Company”.
Further information:
Karelian Diamond Resources plc
Brendan McMorrow, Chairman / Maureen Jones, Managing Director

Chairman’s Statement
Dear Shareholder,
I have great pleasure in presenting the Company’s Half-Yearly Report and condensed Financial Statements for the period ended 30 November 2025.
The Company has been carefully progressing the potential of its assets during the period.
Northern Ireland – exploring Nickel, Copper, Platinum Group Elements
The Company holds a significant licence package covering over 1000 km2 in Northern Ireland where it has established first mover advantage and continues to advance its exploration programme exploring for Nickel, Copper and Platinum Group Elements.
Significant work was carried out by Dr Larry Hulbert, a geological consultant, first engaged in early 2024, who carried out an investigation of the licence areas and built a comprehensive database on them incorporating geology, geochemistry, geophysics, remote sensing, and base Geographic Information System (GIS) information including TELLUS data and gravity geophysical data.
Dr Hulbert’s report confirmed significant potential for Nickel, Copper and Platinum Group Elements and recommended a follow up work programme.
The Company has been progressing with follow on work over the last six months including entering into an enterprise partnership with Trinity College Dublin on a work programme jointly funded by Research Ireland to be carried out on the Platinum Group Element potential of the Company’s licence areas in Ireland over the coming years.
In June 2025 the Company announced that it had identified the site of the historic Cappagh Copper Mine as a significant new target for investigation within the relevant licence area (KDR4).
This provides the Company with a tangible copper target in addition to the targets identified by Dr Hulbert’s report and we look forward to commencing a comprehensive exploration programme to unlock the full potential of this area.
Diamond exploration in Finland
While the market sentiment remains challenging from a diamond investment perspective, the Company is focussing its efforts on carefully progressing exploration work, positioning the Company well for a recovery in the retail sentiment towards natural stones.
The Company’s view is that in a global market segment for natural diamonds that is worth over US$29 billion annually, sustainably produced, traceable European diamonds will command a premium.
This premium would be supported by the potential for the Company’s assets to produce coloured diamonds.
Exploration work in the Kuhmo region
Exploration work has continued in the Kuhmo region in Finland with the Company focussing in particular

company_30s.what_they_do: KDR Ltd is a mineral exploration firm that hunts for rare pink‑diamond deposits in northern Finland.  It focuses on identifying kimberlite‑hosted ore bodies, drilling for confirmation, and eventually moving toward a commercial mine if the geology proves viable.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Pink diamonds are a luxury niche with very limited supply and high resale value.  A single confirmed source can drive a significant price premium for the company, giving investors a shot at outsized returns compared to the broader mining sector.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the exploration phase, with no proven mine yet.  It has recently raised modest capital and filed a mining concession, but its cash burn remains high and revenue is zero.  The stock trades at a fraction of the valuation implied by the potential pink‑diamond discovery.

rns_narrative: KDR has been busy tightening its corporate structure: an AGM, disclosure of shareholdings, and a new mining concession that formally authorises drilling in a promising kimberlite area.  These moves signal that the management is actively working to turn the exploration project into a funded, operational asset.
The company’s latest filings also show a modest fundraising round and a copper‑target announcement, suggesting it’s diversifying its exploration portfolio to manage risk.
Together, these events paint a picture of a company that is still in the early‑stage, but that has secured a key regulatory milestone and is preparing for the next funding push.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – the chatter is sparse but the few bullish voices focus on the pink‑diamond potential.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are pointing to the mining concession and the historic pink‑diamond discovery as catalysts that could unlock a huge upside if the drill results confirm the deposit.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue that the company has no revenue, high costs, and a thin cash runway – a classic exploration risk that could lead to further declines or even a winding‑up.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from indifferent to cautiously optimistic after the concession was issued, but overall engagement remains low, keeping the market largely silent.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, giving the stock a surprise‑potential advantage if a catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming AGM will reveal the board’s stance on the mining concession and any new capital‑raising plans.
A drill‑hole report confirming pink‑diamond grade or a copper target hit would validate the geological model.
A surge in trading volume around the AGM or drill announcement would signal market confidence and could trigger a breakout.
If copper prices rise above $8,000 per tonne, the company’s copper‑target could become a secondary revenue source, adding upside.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to watch over the next 2‑3 months: a volume spike that breaks the 20‑day average, the price touching the 52‑week low support, and any positive drill‑hole data or new funding announcement.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early phase of a deep‑crash cycle – capitulation appears complete, but the mean‑reversion engine has not yet been triggered.  The APEX score indicates tight compression, so a breakout is likely if a catalyst appears.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The 86 % drop puts the price at a very low point.  If the pink‑diamond discovery is validated, the stock could recover to 50 % of its pre‑crash peak, giving roughly a 100 % gain from now.  Even a modest rebound to 70 % of the peak would still produce a 70 % upside – a clear asymmetric reward.  The signal hierarchy classifies this as an ORANGE (extreme crash) event, historically delivering >100 % returns when a catalyst arrives.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still has no revenue and a thin cash runway.  If the drill results are negative or if it fails to raise further capital, the price could stay near the current level or decline further into a new low.  The realistic worst‑case is a 86 % loss from the peak, which is the current drawdown.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Upside ≈ 100 % vs downside 86 % – a risk‑reward ratio of roughly 1.2:1.  The upside is larger than the downside, making this a classic asymmetric play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 52‑week low or just above the recent support level, size a position that risks no more than 5 % of trading capital, and set a stop 5 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the AGM or a positive drill‑hole report.  If the price touches the 52‑week low support and volume surges, consider a quick entry.  If the stock remains flat or falls further, stay out.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a delisting or declares bankruptcy, exit immediately.  No other terminal risk is evident at present.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep‑crash at 86 % with a silent market and a potential pink‑diamond catalyst makes this a high‑asymmetry contrarian play – the upside outpaces the downside if the company can validate its exploration promise.
